picks.reasoning: Best edge on the board. DG fair +870, DK +1000 = 13.5% positive EV. Same mispricing appears in his Top 10 (+425 DK, +8.3% EV) and outright win (+5500 DK, +6.5% EV). Also the free pick this week.

Only book above DG fair in the Top 5 market — DK +660 vs. fair +640 (+2.7% EV). Won the Schwab Challenge at Colonial this season, a near-identical course-fit profile (App-heavy, Putt-heavy). Ranks #27 in SG: Approach.

Best EV of any Top 10 bet on the board. DG fair +805, FD +900 = 10.5% positive EV. Same mispricing in Top 20 (FD +340, +7.6% EV). Ranks #25 in SG: Approach. Market consistently underrates him relative to the model.

One of two players in the field on both the top-33 SG: Approach (#18) and top-32 SG: Putting (#25) lists. TPC River Highlands rewards both (App 28%, Putt 32%). Near-fair at +175 (DG fair +185); this is a course-fit conviction bet, not a market-edge bet.

Ranks #9 in SG: Approach in this field. DG fair +971, FD +1000 = 2.7% positive EV. Quietly solid season — had a Top 10 at the PGA Championship (+800, which hit). At the +1000 cap, the approach profile and mild model edge justify the inclusion.
Best EV in the shorter-price tier at 6.7%. DG fair +237, Caesars +260. Approach #6 in this field — same case as the longer-priced plays above, just a wider net at proportionally shorter odds.

Approach #8 in this field. DG fair +295, FD +300 = 1.2% positive EV. Slim edge but real course fit — 28% approach weight suits his profile. Missed the cut at the U.S. Open but consistent elsewhere this season.

Approach #2 in this field. DG fair +156, FD +160 = 1.4% positive EV. Wider net version of the Spaun case — he just needs a top-20 finish rather than a top-10. Lower variance entry on the same course-fit profile.
